subject: [PATCH v2 4/4] memcg: multi objcg charge support

reviews.author: Muchun Song
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Muchun Song requested that the patch author update the comment numbering to match the actual sequence, noting that it was incorrect.

Reviewer Muchun Song noted that the patch does not handle the case where a CPU has multiple objcg variants of the same memcg, and requested that the patch be modified to handle this scenario.
reviews.raw_body:
^
           4

Since you're already modifying the comments in this section,
would you mind fixing the numbering as well? I noticed that the
sequence was wrong a while back :)

---

^
           5

Thanks,
Muchun

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch needs further revision and is willing to either fix it in a future version or request someone else (Andrew) to make the necessary changes.
sentiment_signals: willingness to fix or ask for help
reviews.raw_body:
Haha I didn't even notice. If I send a new version, I will fix this otherwise I
will ask Andrew to fix inplace.

2026-05-22.patch_summary: This patch addresses a performance regression caused by the recent change to split a memcg's single obj_cgroup into one per NUMA node. The patch introduces multi-slot support for obj_stock_pcp, which caches cached_objcg variants of one memcg plus a few siblings without forcing a drain. This is achieved by turning nr_bytes and cached_objcg into arrays, scanning all slots on consume/refill/account, and evicting a random slot only when full. The patch also optimizes the layout to minimize cache line touches.
